zh.Ashton-Under-Line, Metropolitan Burrough: 英國英格蘭格亞特 曼徹斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Holland I於1420年出生在英國英格蘭格亞特 曼徹斯特時，他的父親Thurston de Holland III30歲，母親Margaret de Warrene11歲。他和Elizabeth de Southworth至少有1個兒子。他去世於1483年2月7日在英國英格蘭享年63歲。

zh-hans.Ashton-Under-Line, Metropolitan Burrough: 英国英格兰格亞特 曼徹斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de Holland I于1420年出生在英国英格兰格亞特 曼徹斯特时，他的父亲Thurston de Holland III30岁，母亲Margaret de Warrene11岁。他和Elizabeth de Southworth至少有1个儿子。他去世于1483年2月7日在英国英格兰享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Holland I was born in 1420, in Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thurston de Holland III, was 30 and his mother, Margaret de Warrene, was 11. He had at least 1 son with Elizabeth de Southworth. He died on 7 February 1483, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Holland I est né en 1420, à Greater Manchester, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thurston de Holland III, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Warrene, avait 11 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Elizabeth de Southworth. Il est décédé le 7 février 1483, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Holland I 1420 in Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thurston de Holland III 30 und seine Mutter Margaret de Warrene 11. Er hatte mit Elizabeth de Southworth mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 7. Februar 1483 im Alter von 63 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Holland I è nato il nel 1420 nel Greater Manchester, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thurston de Holland III, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Warrene, aveva 11 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Elizabeth de Southworth. Morì il 7 febbraio 1483, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Holland I(이)가 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 그리터 먼체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thurston de Holland III(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Warrene(은)는 11세였다. 그는 Elizabeth de Southworth(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1483년 2월 7일 영국 잉글랜드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Elizabeth de Southworth. Ele faleceu em 7 de fevereiro de 1483, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Holland I родился в 1420 г. в местности гритир манчестир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thurston de Holland III было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Warrene, 11. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Elizabeth de Southworth. Он умер 7 февраля 1483 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет.
